title: Helianthemum baschkirorum
taxonomy: Helianthemum baschkirorum is classified within the family Cistaceae and the genus Helianthemum. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. The species further falls under the class Magnoliopsida and the order Malvales. Helianthemum baschkirorum is native to Russia, specifically in the Bashkortostan region. This classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological position.
uses: Helianthemum baschkirorum has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Russia, for its potential health benefits. Its leaves, flowers, and roots are said to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. The plant is also valued for its ability to improve digestion and boost the immune system.
